originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Big News from Djibouti! A Long-Time Leader Stays On
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids!

Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here with exciting news from a special part of our big continent. Today, we're flying over to a small, warm country called Djibouti! Get ready to learn something new.

What is a President?

Every country needs a leader, just like your school has a head teacher or your family has a mom or dad who helps make big decisions. In many countries, this main leader is called a 'President'. A president helps decide things for the whole country. They help make sure schools are built, hospitals are working, and people are safe. It's a very big job! Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the whole world! Djibouti is just one of its many wonderful countries!

Meet President Guelleh

In Djibouti, the leader is a man named Mr. Ismail Omar Guelleh. He is 78 years old. That's a lot of birthdays! Mr. Guelleh has been the president of Djibouti for a very, very long time. Can you guess how long? He has been president for 27 years! That's much longer than you've been alive, and even longer than many of your teachers or parents have been alive! Did you know? 27 years is like living through 27 Christmases or Eid celebrations! That's a lot of celebrations for one person to be the leader!

What Happened in the Election?

Once in a while, countries have something called an 'election'. An election is like a big game where people choose who they want to be their leader. When people vote, they mark a paper or press a button to show who they want. Each mark is called a 'vote'. It's like everyone in a class voting for their favorite fruit. Recently, the people of Djibouti had an election. And guess what? Mr. Guelleh won again! He won by a super big amount! Almost everyone who voted, about 97 or 98 out of every 100 people, chose him. Did you know? In some countries, if you are old enough, voting is a special right that helps everyone have a say in choosing their leaders!

What is a "Term"?

When a president wins, they usually get to be the leader for a certain number of years. We call this time a 'term'. It's like playing a game for a set amount of time. Mr. Guelleh has won for the sixth time! This means he will be president for another 'term'. Winning six times in a row is very unusual for a president in many places! Did you know? In some countries, a president can only serve for two terms, which means they can only be the leader for a few years, and then someone new has to be chosen.

Why Didn't Others Play?

There were other people who also wanted to be president of Djibouti. These people are called 'candidates'. It's like having many players who want to be the team captain. But here's something important: most of these other candidates decided not to take part in the election. When people decide not to join in a game or a vote, it's called a 'boycott'. They said they didn't think the election was fair, so they chose not to play. It's like if some players felt the rules of a game were not fair, so they sat out. Did you know? People have different ideas about how a country should be run, and that's why there are often many candidates in an election!

Where is Djibouti?

Djibouti is a small but very special country on the east coast of Africa. It sits right by the Red Sea, which is part of a very big ocean! It's a very hot and sunny place, often feeling like summer all year round! People there love the warm weather. Djibouti has beautiful beaches and super salty lakes. It even has volcanoes that are still bubbling! And under the water, there are amazing colorful fish and corals. Did you know? Djibouti is sometimes called the 'Pearl of the Gulf' because it's so pretty and important for ships that travel between Europe, Asia, and Africa!

Life in Djibouti

Djibouti City is the capital, and it's a very busy place! It has a big port where many ships come and go, bringing goods from all over the world. The people of Djibouti are known to be very friendly and welcoming. They speak languages like French and Arabic, but they also have their own special languages. So, Mr. Guelleh will continue to lead Djibouti. People will be watching to see how he helps his country grow and do well during his next term. Did you know? Many countries in Africa have beautiful, busy capital cities, just like Djibouti City!

A Big Lesson for Us

This news from Djibouti helps us learn about how leaders are chosen in different parts of our world. It teaches us about elections, and about people's choices. It also makes us think about how important it is for everyone to feel that games are fair and that everyone has a chance to play and be heard. So, keep your eyes and ears open, young news explorers! There's always something new to learn about our amazing continent, Africa, and the world!
summary: In the African country of Djibouti, Mr. Guelleh has won to be president again for a very long time, but some other people who wanted to be president did not take part in the voting because they felt the election was not fair.
whyMatters: This news is important because it shows how leaders are chosen in different countries, and it makes us think about what it means for everyone in Djibouti when one person leads for so many years.

originalTitle: Arsenal shocked by Bournemouth, offering Man City Premier League lifeline
title: A Big Surprise in the Football World!
content:
Hello, Amazing Friends!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends from all over Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to tell you about exciting things happening far away, but still super interesting to us! We love football so much here, don't we? It's a game that brings so much joy and excitement! Today, we're going to talk about a big football game that happened in a country called England. It's where some of the world's most famous teams play. Imagine a huge playground where many teams come to kick a ball and score goals! That's kind of like what we're talking about.

What Happened in the Big Game?

There are two very famous football teams we need to talk about. One is called **Arsenal**, and the other is called **Bournemouth**. Last weekend, these two teams played a game. And guess what? There was a big surprise! Usually, Arsenal is a very strong team. Many people thought they would win easily. But Bournemouth, who are also a good team, played with all their might! It was a very exciting match for everyone watching.

Goals, Goals, Goals!

In football, the team that scores more goals wins, right? Well, in this game, Arsenal scored 1 goal. But Bournemouth scored 2 goals! So, the score was 2-1. This means Bournemouth won the game. Hooray for Bournemouth! It was a big deal because Arsenal was playing at their own big stadium. When a team plays 'at home', it means they play in their own special playground, with all their fans cheering very, very loudly for them. It must have been a bit sad for Arsenal's fans when their team didn't win, but that's how sports go sometimes! **Did you know?** The first footballs were made from things like animal bladders or even sewn-up clothes! Now, they are made from special strong materials that help players kick them far and fast.

Playing at Home, Away from Home

So, when we say Arsenal lost 'at home', it means they were playing in their very own big stadium. It’s a special place called Emirates Stadium. Imagine your favourite team playing right in front of your house, with everyone from your village cheering! That's what 'at home' feels like for these big teams. When a team plays 'away', it means they travel to another team's stadium. It's often harder to win when you are playing away because the other team's fans are cheering so much for them!

What is the Premier League?

These teams, Arsenal and Bournemouth, play in something called the **Premier League**. Think of it like a very big competition for the best football teams in England. It's like a special championship that goes on for many months! Every team wants to win the Premier League trophy. Teams play against each other twice in the whole competition. Once at home, and once away. Then, at the very end, the team with the most points wins the whole competition! It's like a long, long race to see who is the champion. **Did you know?** The Premier League is watched by billions of people all around the world, even right here in Africa! Many children in Africa dream of playing in the Premier League one day.

Points Make Prizes!

In the Premier League, teams get 'points' for their games. If a team wins a game, they get 3 points. If they draw (meaning it's a tie, like 1-1 or 0-0), they get 1 point. If they lose, they get 0 points. Simple, right? Before this game, Arsenal was leading the race, meaning they had more points than almost everyone else. They were 9 points ahead of another super strong team called **Manchester City** (or Man City for short). When Arsenal lost to Bournemouth, their points didn't go up. So, the gap stayed at 9 points. Imagine you are running a race, and you are 9 steps ahead of your friend. If you stop for a moment, and your friend keeps running, they might catch up! That's kind of what's happening here.

What Does "Games in Hand" Mean?

Now, here's where it gets really interesting! The news says that Man City has "two games in hand." What does that mean? It means Man City has played fewer games than Arsenal. They still have two extra games they haven't played yet! It's like having two more chances to score points. If Man City wins both of those extra games, they will get 6 extra points (3 points for each win). If they do that, then the gap between them and Arsenal will become much smaller. Instead of being 9 points behind, they could be only 3 points behind! That's a huge difference! So, even though Arsenal is still ahead by 9 points right now, Man City has a chance to catch up because they have those extra games to play. This makes the competition very exciting until the very last day! **Did you know?** Many famous African footballers have played in the Premier League, making us all very proud! Players like Didier Drogba, Yaya Touré, and Mohamed Salah are heroes for many children.

Who are Arsenal and Man City?

Arsenal is a very old and famous club, known for its red shirts. They have won the Premier League many times before. Man City also wears blue shirts and has become one of the best teams in the world in recent years, winning lots of trophies. Both teams have amazing players who kick the ball with incredible skill and speed. They train very hard every day to become the best they can be. It takes a lot of teamwork and practice to be a top football player!

Why Does This Matter to Our Teams?

This news is important because it tells us that the race to win the Premier League is still very, very close! Even when a strong team like Arsenal seems far ahead, another team like Man City can still catch up. It shows that in football, just like in life, you should never give up! Every game counts. It makes the rest of the Premier League season super thrilling to watch. Who will win? We'll have to wait and see! But one thing is for sure: there will be more exciting goals and big surprises.

Keep Cheering!

So, my lovely friends, this is why the game between Arsenal and Bournemouth was such a big deal. It made the football race even more exciting! Remember, whether your team wins or loses, it's always important to cheer them on and enjoy the beautiful game. Keep practicing your own football skills and have fun! That's all for today from your friendly reporter. See you next time for more amazing news from our world! Bye for now!

originalTitle: US-Iran talks on ending war begin in Pakistan
title: US-Iran talks on ending war begin in Pakistan
content: Discussions between Iran and US delegations begin following separate bilateral meetings with Pakistani prime minister.

originalTitle: Al Jazeera’s Diplomatic Editor outlines key issues in US-Iran talks
title: Big Countries Talk It Out! News from Afar!
content:
Hello everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super important news all the way from Africa! I hope you're all having a wonderful day, playing, learning, and sharing smiles. Today, we have some special news about two big countries far, far away. Imagine you and your friend have a little disagreement, maybe about which game to play or how to share a toy. What do you do? You talk about it, right? You use your words to find a way to be friends again and play happily. Well, guess what? Big countries do that too!

Two Countries, USA and Iran

There are two very big countries called the USA (which stands for the United States of America) and Iran. They are very far from us here in Africa, but they are important countries in our big world. Think of them like two very important neighbours in our global village. Sometimes, neighbours have different ideas or worries, and it's good when they can sit down and chat. Now, a grown-up who helps us understand important news, kind of like your teacher explaining things, told us something interesting. His name is James. He works for a news channel called Al Jazeera, which tells stories from all over the world, including our beautiful continent of Africa.

Getting Ready to Talk in Pakistan

James told us that people from the USA and people from Iran are going to meet up. They are going to travel to another country called Pakistan! Can you imagine travelling all that way just to talk? It shows how important it is for them to chat. Pakistan is a beautiful country, also far away, with friendly people and amazing places. It's like choosing a special, quiet place to talk to your friend when you have something important to discuss, away from all the noisy games. Did you know? The capital city of Pakistan is called Islamabad! It's a big city with lots of tall buildings and busy streets, just like some big cities we have here in Africa!

What Are They Talking About?

So, what are they going to talk about? Well, sometimes, countries have different ideas about how things should work in the world. They might have different opinions on certain rules or plans. These differences can sometimes feel a bit tricky, like when you and your friend both want the last slice of cake! James told us there are some 'sticking points.' That's a grown-up way of saying there are some things they don't quite agree on yet. It means they need to work harder to understand each other and find solutions. It's not always easy, but talking is always the best first step. Imagine if you and your friend couldn't decide what game to play. If you just stopped talking, you wouldn't play anything! But if you talk, maybe you can find a new game you both like, or take turns playing what each of you wants. That's what these countries are trying to do – find a way to play nicely together in the big world.

Why Talking is Super Important

Why is it so important for countries like the USA and Iran to talk? Because when countries talk, they learn more about each other. They can share their feelings and ideas. This helps them avoid bigger problems later on. It's like building a strong bridge of understanding between them. When countries talk and understand each other, it helps make the whole world a safer and happier place for everyone. It means fewer worries and more chances for all people, including us kids, to grow up in peace and friendship. Just like when you make up with a friend, everyone feels better and can play again! Did you know? When countries talk to each other to solve problems and make friends, it's called 'diplomacy'! It's a very important job for clever grown-ups who are good at listening and finding fair solutions.

Our African Message of Peace

Here in Africa, we know a lot about talking and sharing. We have many different cultures and languages, but we always find ways to live together, share stories, and help each other. We believe that talking is the key to solving problems, big or small. Ubuntu is a special word from South Africa that means 'I am because we are,' reminding us that we are all connected and need to care for each other. So, when we hear about the USA and Iran getting ready to talk, it makes us happy! It shows that even when things are a bit difficult, people are trying their best to find common ground. It's like when our parents teach us to use our words and our kindness to solve problems with our friends and siblings. Did you know? The United Nations is like a super big club where almost all the countries in the world are members! They meet often to talk about important global problems and find peaceful ways to solve them. It's a wonderful example of countries working together! Let's all send our good thoughts to the people from the USA and Iran. We hope their talks go well in Pakistan, and that they can find happy solutions for everyone. Because when countries are friends, the whole world is a brighter place for all of us! This is your friendly African news reporter signing off. Remember to always talk things out with your friends and family. It makes a big difference! Bye for now!

originalTitle: Frontrunner for California governor denies sexual assault allegations
title: A Kind Leader Says 'That's Not True!'
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! It's your friendly reporter from Africa, here to share a story from far, far away. Today, we are going to a place called California. It's a big, sunny state in a country called America.

Meet Our Friend Who Wants To Lead

In California, there is a kind man who wants to be a very important leader. He is very good at helping people and making new ideas. Many people like him very much. He is like the best runner in a race, trying to win a big prize. He wants to be the 'Governor' of California. That's a very big and important job!

What Is a Governor?

Do you know what a Governor does? Imagine your head teacher at school, or the wise chief of your village. A Governor is like the big boss for a whole state! They help make sure schools are good, roads are safe for everyone to travel, and that all the people in the state are happy and have what they need. It is a very big responsibility to be a Governor. Did you know? In many African countries, we have presidents or prime ministers who are like the big bosses for the whole country! It's a bit like a Governor, but for a much bigger area.

Time For a Special Election

Right now, the people in California are choosing who they want to be their next Governor. Lots of kind and smart people want this important job. Our story is about one man who wants to be the Governor very much. He has been working hard to tell everyone his plans. He wants to make California an even better place for all the families and children.

When People Say Things

Now, sometimes in life, people say things about other people. Maybe it's a good thing, maybe it's something that makes you feel sad. In this story, some people said some things about our friend, the man who wants to be Governor. They said things that made him feel very sad and worried. He heard all the things they were saying about him.

He Said 'That's Not True!'

When our friend heard these things, he stood up tall and strong! He said, 'No, that is not true! What they are saying about me is not correct at all!' When someone says 'No, that's not true!' about something, it's called 'denying' it. It means they really, really believe it didn't happen. Our friend told everyone that he would show them the truth. He said he would fight with 'facts' to prove what he was saying.

What Are 'Facts'?

What exactly are 'facts'? Facts are like clues in a treasure hunt! They are things that are truly, truly real and can be proven. If someone says, 'The sky is green,' but you look up and see it's blue, then 'the sky is blue' is a fact! Facts help us know what is truly, truly the truth. They are like strong building blocks of what is real. They help us understand what really happened. Our friend who wants to be Governor wants to gather all the facts. He wants to show everyone that what was said about him is simply not true. Did you know? Scientists use facts to learn amazing things about our world, like why the sun is hot or how trees grow so tall!

Why Facts Are So Important For Leaders

It is very important for leaders, like our friend who wants to be Governor, to always tell the truth. We need to trust them completely. And it's also important for everyone to look at the facts carefully before deciding if something is true or not. We need to be fair and listen to everyone's story. But most importantly, we must always follow the facts to find the real story and understand what is true.

Being Fair to Everyone

Imagine if someone said you broke a toy, but you know you didn't! You would want to show everyone the truth, wouldn't you? You would want them to listen to your side of the story and look for clues to prove you were telling the truth. That is exactly what our friend is doing. He wants everyone to be fair and to look at all the facts before making up their minds. He wants to clear his name.

A Story About Trust

This story from California is really about trust. We need to trust our leaders to be good and to always tell the truth. When someone says something bad, and the leader says it's not true, we need to carefully look at the facts to understand everything. It helps us build a world where everyone can trust each other and know that the truth will always come out. We learn to be careful listeners and thinkers.

Our African Wisdom

In Africa, we always say that the truth is like the sun. No matter how many clouds there are, the sun always shines brightly in the end! Let's hope the truth shines brightly for everyone in California and helps them choose a kind and honest leader.
summary: A kind man who wants to be a big leader in California says that some bad things people said about him are not true, and he wants to show everyone the real facts to prove his innocence.
whyMatters: It's very important for everyone to know the truth about people who want to be leaders, and to always look at the facts carefully before deciding what is real and who to trust.
